entry.title: 3 more videos on integrating SAP Build Apps with DMS
entry.content:
I wrote a blog about integrating Document Management Service (DMS) with SAP Build Apps, which I think will be especially helpful for those of you working on apps that involve document management.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/connect-sap-build-apps-with-dms-free-projects-videos/ba-p/13986096

The blog includes:

  • Free project
  • Links to 3 new videos showing how to connect with DMS using the new HTTP Destination Request flow function, including with the help of the free project
  • Additional tips on DMS and on using the new flow function

I’d love to hear how you’re using DMS in your own SAP Build Apps projects or any feedback you have. Feel free to join the conversation here or on the blog!

entry.title: Re: How to read environment variable of project of SAP Build Process Automation in Script Task
entry.content: I'll let @weiss_zhao1 speak for themselves, but the post talked about Script Task in process, but the solution talked about Custom Script in automation.

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Lucerne, Switzerland)
entry.content: Thanks for pointing out. I am testing and checking ... on our QA site it looks fine (same page) so will have to investigate. I have republished Step 1 in case it was just a publishing hiccup. Will update.

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Lucerne, Switzerland)
entry.content:
Step 1 seems to be fixed ... please check.

I will work on Step 4.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps: how to display a pdf document within a container?
entry.content:
• You are starting with a URL to a PDF?
  • Web or mobile or both?

entry.title: Re: List Creation, Population and Retrieval
entry.content: Use these activities to manage your list: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/list-management?locale=en-US&q=list+variable

entry.title: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation has introduced the "Wait for an API Call" step, that pauses a process instance and waits for some system – any system – to call a specific API to resume the process instance. 

NOTE: The step was scheduled to be introduced this week and next to all the SAP BTP landscapes, but there are no guarantees, and the release could be delayed.

 

Why Use It

I believe the idea behind this is a scenario where, from your process, you call an external system that must execute some task before the rest of the process resumes.

For example:

  • A process receives an event from S/4HANA of a new employee/BP.
  • Some approval steps and other steps are executed.
  • Once approved, the process calls a Badge system to create an entry badge for the new person to get into the premises.
  • That Badge system does what it does.
  • When the Badge system is finished, it calls the API connected to the "Wait for an API Call" step, which resumes the process. 

P.S.: This is the idea behind our new SAP Build CodeJam.

 

How It Works

Add "Wait for an API Call" Step

Simply click the + sign to add a step, and go to Controls and Events area.

Then select the Wait for an API Call step.

You then give the trigger a name.

That's all you need to do.

If you want the API call that resumes the process to include additional data, you can define "input" parameters. The UI is the same as when specifying Process Inputs.

 

Create API Key

When calling the API for resuming the process instance, you must provide an API key with the proper permissions.

Go to the Control Tower, enter your environment, and select API Keys. 

Click Add API Key.

  1. Give the key a name.
  2. Give the key the following scopes:
    • trigger_read
    • trigger_execute

When calling the API, you must send the API key under a header called irpa-api-key.

 

Call the API 

Once you deploy the process to an environment, a separate trigger is created in that environment for that step -- in the same way an API trigger is created to trigger a new process instance.

Go into your environment, then to the Triggers tab. You'll see a trigger for your wait step.

Select the trigger and click View.

This will show you API details that are slightly different than the API details for the standard process API trigger.

The URL:

  • Standard API Trigger: A fixed URL (for each landscape). For EU10, for example, it is https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-eu-prod.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances
  • Wait Trigger: The URL path is different and it includes a unique GUID that defines that specific trigger. If you create more than one Wait trigger in a process, they will have different GUIDs. I am not aware of a way to get this GUID at runtime.

The payloads are also different.

  • Standard API Trigger: Contains a definitionId (plus all the process inputs) so the system knows which process you want to trigger.
  • Wait Trigger: Contains an executionId (plus any inputs defined for the Wait trigger) so the system knows which process instance needs to be resumed.

That API can only be used if the specified process instance is actually waiting for that specific API call.

Remember to send your API key under the irpa-api-key header.

 

Getting the Process Instance ID 

You will need to send the process instance ID to the 3rd-party system that will resume the process instance, so they can include it in the request body of the API.

One way to do this is to create a custom variable to hold the instance ID.

Add Script Task to get the instance ID from the info object and put it in the custom variable.

 

Deleting Wait Triggers

A kind of quirky behavior occurs when you delete a "Wait" trigger and then redeploy – AND you have process instances still running from the previous version of your process.

👉If you delete the "Wait" trigger, what happens to those process instances?

Well, no worries. 

The old processes will continue to run and the old "Wait" trigger can still be used. It will continue to appear in the Control Tower.

When the last process that uses that trigger is completed, that "Wait" trigger will disappear from the Control Tower.

 

Errors

I have not re-created all the potential errors, but so far have figured out when the following status codes are returned when calling the "Wait" API.

  • 204: Success
  • 400: The GUID in the URL does not represent an existing "Wait" trigger, or you did not send an API key.
  • 403: The process instance ID passed in the request body does not exist or that process instance is not waiting for an API call.

This is just based on a few minutes testing, so it may not be 100% accurate. And there may be cases when the API returns other status codes. If you experience any, please let us all know.

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: I will delve into the code, but I get what you sent in your post when I run it. What am I supposed to get?

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: Not the same?

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content:
I created a button and run a JS flow function with your code:

 

I added the code (return) so that the flow function would output the answer to the next flow functions, but it even worked without.

entry.title: Re: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
Thanks for comments.

When you say agent, I am assuming you are talking about automation (iRPA). But this is a process step, not an automation activity. My understanding is that the process instance completely stops until the API call is made.

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: I took your code as is, with the input inside the JS. So I assume your issue is something of how the input is getting inside the JS flow function. But is this JS in SAP Build Apps project or in SAP Build Process Automation project?

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: Might help to see more of that JS flow and the way you are getting it inside the next JS flow.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Publishing Public Link
entry.content:
As far as I understand, deployed apps from SAP Build Apps -- deployed directly to the Work Zone approuter -- require SAP BTP authentication.

You could build an mtar and download it, and then modify it perhaps, and redeploy -- but my understanding is still the basis of the whole system is that SAP BTP must identify the user. More info perhaps here: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/build-and-deploy-web-applications

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content:
I created this app to test your scenario, but with a simplified API.

In the first I fetch the Customers from Northwind, and return the first one (an object).

In the next, I take as the input the output of the previous JavaScript -- JSON object. ANd just to demonstrate I have an object and can work on it like an object, I display in the console the keys and values. I was also able to select specific fields in the object.

I can see the output in the console.

All this to demonstrate that the passing of JSON works between JS flow functions. Previously, we showed that your code also works in the JavaScript flow function.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: How to hide the page Tab in Build apps Preview page
entry.content:
I believe the way you do this is by creating custom navigation (i.e., the default navigation does not support this).

Here is a blog by @marihirvi: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/create-on-brand-navigation-experiences-in-sap-build-apps-using-custom/ba-p/13617392

Essentially you could do 2 ways, both involve disabling the default navigation.

  • You can create a container the contains icons or text that are the navigation menu, and set visibility to true/false based on whatever you decide, for example, some page variable that tracks the user's role. We did this here in our challenge 2 years ago -- and you can even download it to see it: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-discussions/april-citizen-developer-challenge-sap-build-apps-task-0/m-p/13654312
  • You can use the marketplace component "Responsive navigation bar" with the custom navigation lists you can create (go to Settings > Navigation). Create a list for each role and then display that navigation based on the user's role. Essentially the same as the first but you hide/show an entire navigation bar. I don't like this one so much.

All of this is my understanding, but Mari will likely know more.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
@VaradBhagwat The second 2 screenshots are hard to read.

1) Where are you sending the credentials?

2) We usually solve CORS by creating a proxy service, or more simply using BTP destinations

entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps does not currently have role-based access, except to VCF. Some possible ideas:

  • If you want to use BTP roles, you could create a CAP service that defines roles, assign those roles to users as you would with any other roles in the cockpit, and then when your app loads see the roles and hide the UIs as needed. This is described in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-app.html#0bb3011a-41c3-40dc-90d3-5084d2fab47b
  • You can use the role based access in VCF to allow an admin to manage a table(s) with user permissions, and then similarly check these when a user opens the app.

These are just my ideas, and I do not know what would be the recommendation by the product team.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
It doesn't seem you are calling via the destination. My colleague @nicoschoenteich did a 2-minute series on UI5, including this video how he set up access to external API using destination via approuter and to test locally.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MHJzIz7iiHs&list=PL6RpkC85SLQC4kuj22e4hw85Sa1pClD8y&index=4

Check out the entire series and subscribe to SAP Developers.

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: My pleasure 🙂
